compositional_truth_check.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss (No viable mint foliage detected)
compositional_truth_check.P4_Silver_Guest: Present (Shared in Black Pot with P2/P3 area)

inventory_reconciliation.status: Partial failure. P2 (Mexican Mint) is absent/necrosed. P3 and P4 show signs of severe stress and senescence.
inventory_reconciliation.anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments in P4/P3 soil; significant leaf chlorosis and necrosis across all specimens.

plant_audit.P3: Pothos leaf is undergoing rapid senescence (yellowing/browning).
plant_audit.P4: Single leaf remains, showing signs of turgor loss.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears consistently damp/compacted; potential for root rot.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Eggshell fragments present, likely an attempt at calcium supplementation or pH adjustment.
biome_observations.lighting_impact: Insufficient light levels; the cool spectrum LED is not preventing etiolation or decline.

temporal_deltas.Earliest_to_T-4: Initial state shows P3 leaf with edge necrosis.
temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-2: Progressive yellowing of P3 leaf; P2/P4 area shows no recovery.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_Current: P3 leaf has lost significant turgor; P4 leaf is drooping further.

vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of rapid decline. The Pothos (P3) is failing due to what appears to be chronic overwatering or root-zone suffocation, evidenced by the yellowing leaf and lack of structural integrity. The Mexican Mint (P2) has suffered a systemic loss, leaving only bare soil. The remaining specimens are struggling under the current environmental parameters, which are clearly insufficient for their metabolic needs.
